exemplar.title: To what extent has Ivorian government intervention reduced the prevalence of child labour and promoted economic development from 2020 to 2023?

detailedExemplarData.title: To what extent has Ivorian government intervention reduced the prevalence of child labour and promoted economic development from 2020 to 2023?

subject.title: Economics
subject.description:
**IB Economics** is a **Group 3** subject in the **International Baccalaureate Diploma Programme (IBDP)**. It explores **microeconomics, macroeconomics, the global economy, and development economics**, with a strong focus on **analytical and evaluative skills, real-world application of economic theory, and quantitative analysis**.

The course is available at **Standard Level (SL, 150 teaching hours)** and **Higher Level (HL, 240 teaching hours)**, with **first assessment in 2024**.

### Assessment at a Glance

| Assessment | Format | SL | HL | Weighting |
|------------|--------|----|----|-----------|
| **Paper 1** | Extended response questions | 1h 15min | 1h 30min | 30% |
| **Paper 2** | Data response questions | 1h 45min | 1h 45min | 40% (SL), 30% (HL) |
| **Paper 3** | Mathematical questions | N/A | 1h | 20% (HL only) |
| **Internal Assessment** | Portfolio of 3 commentaries | 20 hours | 20 hours | 20% |

strengths: Topic is communicated accurately and effectively with a clear, well-defined focus.
Research question is precisely stated, narrowly scoped and consistently integrated throughout.
Methodology employs an appropriate and relevant range of sources with evidence of informed selection.
weaknesses: Minor typographical and wording imperfections slightly detract from overall polish.
Methodology section lacks explicit discussion of source limitations or reliability evaluation.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The essay meets all descriptors at the top of the 5â6 band: a sharply defined, discipline-appropriate topic; a clearly focused and well-connected research question; and an appropriate, informed selection of methods and sources. Occasional minor language slips prevent any detriment to clarity but do not undermine the investigationâs focus or methodological rigor. Hence the maximum 6â/â6 is justified.

strengths: Demonstrates clear and coherent knowledge of economic theories (externalities, development indicators, market failure).
Terminology and concepts (MPC/MSC, HDI, IHDI, AD/LRAS) are used accurately and consistently.
Applies a range of relevant source material appropriately to support arguments.
weaknesses: Integration of figures and tables is uneven; several visuals lack in-text discussion or clear linkage.
Citation style is inconsistent (footnotes vs. Harvard) and some captions omit sources or time-frame justifications.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The work shows clear disciplinary understanding and accurate use of concepts, meriting the 5â6 band. Gaps in source integration and citation consistency warrant a one-mark reduction from the maximum. Overall, the application of source material and depth of topic knowledge justify a score of 5â/â6.

strengths: Research is generally appropriate, relevant and well aligned with the research question.
Analysis is effective and leads to well-supported conclusions about policy impacts and development trends.
Evaluation acknowledges key data limitations and offers reasoned critique of sources and findings.
weaknesses: Causal links are sometimes asserted without sufficient methodological backing or robustness checks.
Limited discussion of primary data alternatives or triangulation methods to strengthen reliability.
Concluding recommendations could be more concrete and detailed to enhance policy implications.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The investigation demonstrates a well-structured argument with critical evaluation, moving it into the top bracket. While most research is consistently relevant and analysis is sound, the student falls short of full marks by not fully substantiating causal claims, lacking deeper methodological triangulation, and offering broad rather than specific policy recommendations. A score of 10â/â12 reflects these minor but noteworthy limitations.

strengths: Report follows the required extended-essay structure with clear headings and navigable layout.
Tables, figures and textual elements are generally well formatted and legible.
Consistent use of sections (introduction, methodology, analysis, evaluation, conclusion, bibliography) enhances readability.
weaknesses: Table of contents omits page numbers for some entries, reducing navigability.
Reference formatting remains inconsistent across bibliography and in-text citations.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Presentation meets all core structural and formatting conventions and supports clear communication of ideas. Minor lapses in the table of contents and citation consistency are acknowledged but do not significantly impede the reader. Therefore, the full 4â/â4 is awarded.

strandSelections.reasoning: The research question is quoted verbatim several times, is narrow in time frame and scope, and is tightly linked to child-labour prevalence and development indicators; this evidences clarity and focus. Some phrasing (âto what extentâ) still allows evaluative space, confirming suitability for sustained argument. Minor typographical errors prevent the highest degree of polish; therefore the strand is best awarded 5 rather than 6.

The student states a precise, discipline-appropriate topic â the economic implications of child labour in CÃ´te dâIvoire â and links it explicitly to government intervention and development during a defined 2020-23 window. Purpose and focus are reiterated in the introduction, methodology and throughout the headings, showing clear intentionality. Minor wording slips do not impede communication, so the descriptor for 5-6 is met, though the sharpest articulation that would justify the full 6 is slightly lacking.

Tables, graphs and statistics employed are mostly pertinent to the RQ, and major sources are reputable; however, integration is unevenâseveral figures are inserted without clear discussion, and citations in the text are occasionally incomplete. Because relevance is âmostlyâ rather than consistently demonstrated, a solid mid-band score of 3 is appropriate.

The essay follows the required EE structure (title page, contents grid, intro, methodology, analysis, evaluation, conclusion, bibliography) and internal headings aid navigation. Minor omissions (e.g., full abstract, complete page numbers in ToC) prevent maximum marks; thus a 3 within the top band is appropriate.

Basic layout elementsâword count, page numbers, tables/figuresâare present, but image resolution is poor, some captions are incomplete, and reference formatting is inconsistent. These lapses indicate that âsome layout considerations are missing or incorrectâ, corresponding to the 1-2 band with a score of 2.

An evaluation section recognises data limitations, corruption, informality and measurement issues, showing some critical perspective. Nonetheless, alternative explanations, robustness checks and depth of synthesis are modest. The argument is reasoned but only âpartially criticalâ, matching the lower end of the 4-6 band.

The student offers diagrammatic analysis of externalities and AD/LRAS shifts and comments on correlations among indicators. Reasoning is generally logical and conclusions mostly supported, though causal links are asserted more than proven and statistical techniques are limited. Hence the middle of the 4-6 band (5) is warranted.

The student applies economic growth vs. development, market failure, negative externalities, Lorenz curves and PPC convincingly, indicating sound disciplinary knowledge. Explanations are coherent but sometimes descriptive and do not always engage with counter-arguments or advanced literature, placing the work at the top of the 3-4 band.

A wide set of macro and social indicators is marshalled; most are directly tied to the question, demonstrating clear relevance. Yet dependence on secondary sources with acknowledged gaps, and the absence of triangulation, lowers reliability. These features fit the 4-6 band, with evidence strong enough for a score of 5.

The essay outlines reliance on secondary data from NORC, ILO, World Bank, UNICEF, etc., and lists specific indicators to be collected. This shows informed selection, but the range is moderate and there is no discussion of source reliability, limitations of secondary data or alternative primary methods. Data gaps (e.g., child-labour prevalence only 2013 & 2023) appear later without mitigation strategies. Hence the work aligns with âgenerally relevantâ¦ some evidence of informed selectionâ, meriting the upper end of the 3-4 band.

Terminology such as MPC/MSC, HDI vs. IHDI, LRAS/AD, informal economy, etc., is employed accurately and consistently. Occasional spelling errors (âprevelanceâ, âhypnotiseâ) do not obscure meaning. Overall usage meets the 5-6 descriptor, but lapses in proofreading justify awarding 5 rather than 6.

annotations.comment: The research-question label is separated from its text; consider consolidating them on one line or block to improve clarity.
The table of contents omits page numbers for each heading, reducing navigability. Add page references to all entries.
Consider including an abstract on page 2 summarising the research question, methods, and key findings to strengthen the structure.
Footnote citations use superscripts inconsistently and vary in style; adopt a consistent in-text referencing format (e.g., Harvard).
The introduction is descriptive but does not explicitly restate the research question to frame the analysis. Add the RQ here to guide the reader.
The relevance section relies on personal anecdote; strengthen it by linking the topicâs importance to academic literature or economic theory.
The methodology section lists secondary sources but lacks discussion of their limitations or reliability; evaluate why these data suffice.
Figure 1 lacks a source in its caption and spans 2018â2019 outside the core period; include citation and justify time frame.
The title is clearly stated at the start of the report, effectively communicating the investigationâs focus.
A precise word count is provided, satisfying the requirement not to exceed the 4000-word limit.
Page numbering is present in the footer, improving navigation through the extended essay.
There is a typo in the hypothesis (âhypnotiseâ); revise to âhypothesiseâ to ensure accurate use of terminology.
Child-labour prevalence data from 2018 is outside the 2020â2023 research window; either justify its inclusion or restrict to matching periods.
Describe the specific analytical methods (e.g., correlation tests) to demonstrate how you will establish trends and relationships.
The citation â(World bank, 2023)â is inconsistently capitalized; harmonize source details throughout.
The research question is repeated at the top of page 8, which is redundant. Focus here on data presentation instead.
Foreign direct investment graph is not discussed in-text; integrate commentary on its trends and relation to the research question.
The caption âTable 4 number of inspectionsâ¦â lacks capitalization and punctuation; follow consistent table-caption style.
âPrevelanceâ is misspelled in the header of Table 5; correct to âPrevalenceâ for accurate terminology.
Table 1 caption reports 2008â2021 but data cover only 2018â2021; correct caption to match the data.
Graphs on page 9 appear low resolution, making axes and data points hard to read; improve image quality.
Figure 3 caption uses âÎ¼â instead of a clear description of the shift; revise label to âPPC shifts inward from A to Bâ and include source.
The analysis text ends mid-sentence before introducing the market-failure graph; ensure complete transitions and references.
Define all diagram labels (e.g., Qâ, Q*) clearly in the text to ensure readers understand your welfare-loss illustration.
The asymmetric-information discussion cites a supply-demand graph but no figure is provided here; include or remove the reference.
Figure 2 caption lacks source and axis labels; add details to improve interpretability.
The economic-development discussion is very dense; consider breaking it into shorter paragraphs with explicit sub-analyses for clarity.
Figure 4 Lorenz curve lacks axis labels and source citation, limiting its usefulness; add these details.
The evaluation acknowledges data-frequency gaps but could propose alternative primary data methods or triangulation approaches.
Concluding remarks reiterate findings but lack concrete policy recommendations or next steps; consider adding these to strengthen the conclusion.
Bibliography uses mixed citation styles and inconsistent formatting; apply a single style guide across all entries.
